DEFENDANT KENNETH KRAMARZ, TAWONGA JEWISH COMMUNITY CORPORATION's Motion To Seal Records File In Support Of Motion To Enforce Settlement With Plaintiff Mark Rittenberg.

Defendants Kenneth Kramarz and Tawonga Jewish Community Corporation's motion to seal records is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown per CRC 2.550 et seq. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.***

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Attorney, Christine L. Garcia of the law offices of Christine L. Garcia (415.297.3109) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Renee Welze Livingston of the law offices of Livingston Law Firm (925.952.9880) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KENNETH KRAMARZ and TAWONGA JEWISH COMMUNITY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the allegations seeking punitive damages and injunctive relief and denied as to all other allegations sought to be stricken. Mr. Rittenberg has not alleged any facts showing that defendants acted despicably or fraudulently, which are required to support a request for punitive damages. Nor does it appear reasonably possible that Mr. Rittenberg is able to allege facts showing that defendants acted despicably or fraudulently. Mr. Rittenberg's allegations seeking injunctive relief are largely identical to allegations in the first amended complaint, which were determined to be deficient because they entirely concern past conduct. There is no basis to strike any of the other allegations sought to be stricken. Most of those allegations provide permissible background information and none of them are necessarily and clearly false, irrelevant or improper. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Christine L. Garcia of the law offices of Christine L. Garcia (415.297.3109) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Renee Welze Livingston of the law offices of Livingston Law Firm (925.952.9880) appears as counsel for defendants/demurring parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KENNETH KRAMARZ and TAWONGA JEWISH COMMUNITY CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Marc Rittenberg is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. The second amended complaint does not allege a cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and thus the court does not address defendants' demurrer to such a claim. Neither of Mr. Rittenberg's claims are barred by the worker's compensation exclusivity rule because Mr. Rittenberg's allegations do not show either that Mr. Rittenberg is seeking damages for his daughter's work-related injuries or that Mr. Rittenberg's claim necessarily depends on Ms. Rittenberg's injuries. (Snyder v. Michael's Stores, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 991, 1000.) The events alleged to have caused Mr. Rittenberg's injuries occurred after Ms. Rittenberg's death. The death of an employee terminates the employment relationship. (Labor Code S 2920(c).) At this stage of the case, it cannot be determined as a matter of law that defendants owed no duty to Mr. Rittenberg. Defendants' argument that Mr. Rittenberg's claims are barred by the First Amendment is unpersuasive because Mr. Rittenberg's claims do not pose the problem of a civil court attempting to override ecclesiastical edicts. Moreover, at this point, it is far from clear that the court will be called on to resolve a dispute regarding the proper interpretation of Jewish law. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
